reasoning: The document conversion failed catastrophically in its primary purpose: capturing the contract award table. With over 90% of tabular data rows omitted (3 rows captured out of 33) and the presence of fabricated administrative text, the output is functionally useless and requires a complete reprocessing or manual rebuild. These failures meet the explicit criteria for rejection due to majority table destruction.

concerns.description: Fabricated paragraph (0 source overlap): "Roy Cooper, Governor Eddie M. Buffaloe Jr., Secretary Laura H. Hogshead, Director"
concerns.output_quote:
Roy Cooper, Governor
Eddie M. Buffaloe Jr., Secretary
Laura H. Hogshead, Director

concerns.analysis: The 'Contract Award' table in the source document contains 32 project rows on page 1 and 1 additional project row on page 2 (APP-01987), for a total of 33 project data entries. The output JSON table only includes 3 project rows (1, 5, and APP-01987), omitting approximately 30 rows (91% of data rows). This is a critical loss of tabular data.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table on Page 1 contains 32 project rows (Project #1 through #32). Table continuation on Page 2 contains 1 project row (APP-01987).
concerns.output_quote: The JSON table contains only 3 data rows for projects: '1', '5', and 'Replaced APP-09700 above' (APP-01987).

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The 'Amendment Details' section is intended to specify the new contract award details through the provided table. Since the table has lost over 90% of its content, the section fails to convey the substantial majority of the information found in the source document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source contains 33 specific project award entries with detailed cost data.
concerns.output_quote: The output contains only 3 project award entries, losing the vast majority of the amendment's core technical content.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is flawed as it skips H1 and H2 levels entirely. All headings, including the document title and major section headers, are assigned at the H3 level. This creates a flat structure that does not represent the logical hierarchy of the source document, where 'AMENDMENT NUMBER THREE (3)' is clearly the primary title and 'PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE:' is a major section heading.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The document contains a clear title 'AMENDMENT NUMBER THREE (3)' and major sections such as 'PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE:'.
concerns.output_quote: The output uses H3 for the title (element index heading-1) and for major sections (element index heading-5 and heading-6).
concerns.location: Throughout the document headers and section titles.

concerns.analysis: The main contract award table is severely truncated. While the source table spans two pages and includes 32 numbered rows plus multiple summary and replacement rows, the output table only contains 5 data/summary rows in total, skipping rows 2-4 and 6-32. This represents a significant failure in capturing the table's structural organization and complexity.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The table on page 1 contains rows numbered 1 through 32. Page 2 contains additional replacement and summary rows for the same table.
concerns.output_quote: The 'Replaced Contract Award Details' table in the JSON contains only 5 rows: Project 1, Project 5, Replaced APP-09700 above, Total Amended Contract Amount, and the Amendment #3 summary row.
concerns.location: Amendment Details section, 'Replaced Contract Award Details' table.

concerns.description: Fabricated tables: baseline has 0 tables but output has 2

action_items: Verify the source extraction layer to ensure the 2-page table is being read correctly.
Investigate the source of the hallucinated names (Governor/Secretary) to prevent injection of false metadata.
Correct heading hierarchy to reflect document structure (H1 for Title, H2 for Sections).
